user_request: Prepare a suit for permanent injunction restraining Defendant from interfering with Plaintiff's peaceful possession of schedule property. Plaintiff is in lawful possession. Defendant attempting illegal encroachment. Include temporary injunction prayer under Order 39 Rules 1 & 2 CPC. Draft clearly with property description in schedule.

facts.summary: Plaintiff seeks a permanent injunction to restrain the Defendant from interfering with their peaceful possession of a scheduled property. The Plaintiff claims lawful possession, while the Defendant is alleged to be attempting illegal encroachment. A temporary injunction under Order 39 Rules 1 & 2 CPC is also requested.

slots.value: Temporary Injunction under Order 39 Rules 1 & 2 CPC

assumptions: Plaintiff has immediate right to possess the property
Defendant's acts are ongoing or imminent

civil suit court fee calculation injunction

sections.instruction: Draft the suit title line. The title must reflect the PRIMARY RELIEF sought as indicated by the classified doc_type. Use standard Indian court pleading format (all caps, filed under applicable CPC provision). If a monetary amount is the primary relief, include the amount in the title. Otherwise, state the nature of the suit (e.g., 'SUIT FOR [nature of relief]').

sections.heading: BETWEEN
sections.instruction: Draft the parties section listing plaintiff(s) and defendant(s) with full particulars: name, age, occupation, address. Use competency averment (sui juris / competent to sue). If any detail is missing, use {{PLACEHOLDER}}. Adapt party labels to the suit type (e.g., Petitioner/Respondent for writ petitions).

sections.heading: JURISDICTION
sections.instruction: Draft the jurisdiction section establishing territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction based on the facts. State the SPECIFIC factual basis for territorial jurisdiction (where defendant resides, where property is situated, where cause of action arose). State the suit valuation and that it falls within the court's pecuniary limits.

sections.heading: FACTS OF THE CASE
sections.instruction: Draft the facts section as numbered paragraphs in chronological narrative form. Frame the narrative around the PRIMARY RELIEF sought (from the doc_type). Each paragraph should cover ONE event or stage. Reference documents as Annexure labels. Do NOT state legal conclusions — keep it purely factual.

must_include.description: At least one date or date placeholder

sections.heading: LEGAL BASIS AND GROUNDS
sections.instruction: Draft the legal basis section citing statutory provisions and legal principles that support the PRIMARY RELIEF sought (from the doc_type). For EACH provision: (1) State the legal rule it creates, (2) State what conditions trigger it, (3) Show how the facts satisfy those conditions. Cite ONLY provisions from the verified list or those naturally applicable to this doc_type. Include alternative pleas and defensive pleading where appropriate.

must_include.description: At least one statutory provision cited

sections.heading: CAUSE OF ACTION
sections.instruction: Draft the cause of action section in PARAGRAPH prose. Must cover: (1) ORIGIN — when and how the right to sue first arose, (2) FURTHER ACCRUAL — subsequent acts that sustain the right, (3) CONTINUITY — why the cause of action is continuing. Anchor to the actual triggering event from the facts.

sections.heading: LIMITATION
sections.instruction: Draft the limitation section stating: (1) the applicable Article of the Limitation Act 1963 (from enrichment context ONLY — if not provided, use {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} placeholder), (2) the limitation period, (3) the starting date anchored to the cause of action accrual event, (4) that the suit is within time. Do NOT guess limitation articles.

sections.heading: VALUATION AND COURT FEE
sections.instruction: Draft the valuation and court fee section. State the suit valuation, the basis for valuation, and the court fee paid or payable. Use {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} placeholder if the exact amount is not available.

sections.heading: INTEREST
sections.instruction: Draft the interest section with applicable interest claims: pre-suit interest, pendente lite interest, and future interest with rates and legal basis.

must_include.description: Some form of financial relief or interest stated

sections.heading: PRAYER
sections.instruction: Draft the prayer section with specific sub-prayers as lettered items (a, b, c, ...). Each prayer must match the PRIMARY RELIEF of the classified doc_type. Do NOT pray for relief inconsistent with the suit type. Always include costs and a general relief clause 'any other relief the Court deems fit'.

sections.heading: LIST OF DOCUMENTS RELIED UPON
sections.instruction: Generate a numbered list of documents the plaintiff relies upon, mapped to annexure labels. Base the document list on the evidence provided in context. If evidence is empty, list expected documents for this suit type with {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}} placeholders.

sections.heading: VERIFICATION
sections.body:
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of the above plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and the contents based on legal advice are believed to be true on the basis of such advice. No part of this plaint is false and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this {{FILING_DATE}}.

As per Order VI Rule 15 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

filled_sections.heading: BETWEEN
filled_sections.text: BETWEEN The Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}, competent to sue and be sued (sui juris), hereinafter referred to as the Plaintiff, and the Defendant, {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}, residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}, competent to be sued and to sue (sui juris), hereinafter referred to as the Defendant. The Plaintiff is in peaceful and lawful possession of the property described in Schedule A hereto. On or about {{ENCROACHMENT_DATE}}, the Defendant attempted to encroach upon and interfere with the Plaintiff's possession of the Schedule A property, thereby rendering continued interference actionable and justifying the grant of a permanent injunction restraining the Defendant; the Plaintiff also prays for a temporary injunction under Order 39 Rules 1 and 2 CPC to maintain the status quo pending disposal of this suit.

filled_sections.heading: JURISDICTION
filled_sections.text:
Territorial jurisdiction for this suit lies with the Civil Court at {{COURT_PLACE}}, {{STATE}}, as the schedule property is situated at {{PROPERTY_ADDRESS}} within this city, and the cause of action arose at the said property due to the Defendant's interference with the Plaintiff's peaceful possession. The Plaintiff has been in lawful possession of the schedule property since {{POSSESSION_DATE}}. The Defendant resides at {{DEFENDANT_RESIDENCE}} within the jurisdiction, and the Plaintiff resides at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}; on or about {{ENCROACHMENT_DATE}} the Defendant began attempting encroachment on the schedule property, located at {{PROPERTY_ADDRESS}}.

The suit, including the temporary injunction prayer under Order 39 Rules 1 and 2 CPC, is maintainable in this Court because the property and the alleged interference occur within the territorial limits of the said Civil Court. The factual nexus—possession by the Plaintiff, encroachment attempts by the Defendant at the schedule property, and the parties’ residences within the same jurisdiction—establishes the appropriate territorial link for adjudication in this Court.

Pecuniary jurisdiction: The suit seeks relief in the nature of a permanent injunction restraining the Defendant from interfering with Plaintiff’s possession and includes a temporary injunction prayer. The suit value is Rs. {{SUIT_VALUE}}, which falls within the pecuniary limits of this Court. The Court’s monetary jurisdiction for such suits is Rs. {{PECUNIARY_LIMIT}}; accordingly, this Court is empowered to entertain and determine the suit on merits.

filled_sections.heading: FACTS OF THE CASE
filled_sections.text:
1. The Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, is the lawful possessor of the schedule property more particularly described in Schedule-A and situated at {{PROPERTY_ADDRESS}}. The Plaintiff has held peaceful possession since before {{DATE_1}}, and there is documentary evidence of title and possession in Annexure A and Annexure B respectively attached to this plaint.

2. On {{DATE_2}}, the Defendant, {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, with others attempted to encroach upon the schedule property by entering within the boundary and erecting unauthorised structures, thereby interfering with the Plaintiff’s peaceful possession. The encroachment is evidenced by the Site Plan in Annexure C and by photographs in Annexure H, which depict the areas showing interference and construction near the boundary.

3. On {{DATE_3}}, the Plaintiff served a legal notice on the Defendant demanding immediate cessation of the encroachment and maintenance of the Plaintiff’s peaceful possession; the notice is annexed to this pleading as Annexure D.

4. On {{DATE_4}}, the Defendant sent a reply disputing the Plaintiff’s possession and asserting rights to the boundary and the alleged encroached portions; the reply is annexed as Annexure E.

5. On {{DATE_5}}, the Plaintiff filed before the appropriate Court an application for temporary injunction under Order 39 Rules 1 and 2 CPC, seeking to restrain the Defendant from interfering with the Plaintiff’s possession pending the hearing of the suit; the application and supporting affidavits are annexed as Annexure F.

6. On {{DATE_6}}, the Court granted ad-interim relief in favour of the Plaintiff by way of temporary injunction restraining the Defendant from interfering with the Plaintiff’s possession, and this interim order is reflected in Annexure G.

7. As of {{DATE_7}}, the Plaintiff continues in peaceful possession of the schedule property, and there has been no restoration of possession to the Defendant or disturbance by the Defendant beyond the interim measures; this status is evidenced by the continued relevance of Annexure B (Possession Certificate) and Annexure H (Photographs) on record.

8. The facts described herein establish a continuing interference with the Plaintiff’s peaceful possession of the schedule property, as described in Schedule-A and situated at {{PROPERTY_ADDRESS}}, which necessitates the permanent injunction prayed for in this suit so as to prevent any further encroachment or disturbance by the Defendant. Annexures A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H are collectively referred to for documentary support of these events.

filled_sections.heading: LEGAL BASIS AND GROUNDS
filled_sections.text:
The Legal Basis and Grounds for the relief sought proceed on the statutory framework governing suits for permanent injunction and the preservation of rightful possession. Under Section 6 of the CPC, as substituted by the CPC (Amendment) Act, 2002 (22 of 2002), when a suit has been duly instituted, a summons may be issued to the defendant to appear and answer the claim and to file the written statement of his defence within thirty days from the day of institution. In the present case, Plaintiff {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}} has duly instituted a suit in the Court of {{COURT_NAME}} at {{LOCATION}} on {{INSTITUTION_DATE}} against Defendant {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} for a permanent injunction restraining interference with Plaintiff’s peaceful possession of the schedule property described in Schedule hereto, as Defendant is alleged to be attempting illegal encroachment. The Court is thus empowered to issue summons to Defendant requiring appearance and defence within the statutory period, enabling the matter to proceed on the merits and for the Plaintiff to obtain final relief restraining further encroachment.

In respect of future enforcement, Article 136 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963 provides the governing framework for enforcement of a decree for permanent injunction after a prolonged period, with the outer time limit of forty years and related considerations. The present suit seeks a permanent injunction to restrain continuing interference with peaceful possession of the schedule property, the description of which is set out in Schedule herein. The legal consequence is that, should a decree for permanent injunction be required and thereafter sought for enforcement, such enforcement would be governed by Article 136 and the corresponding time limits, and the Plaintiff contends that the instant suit is within the applicable limitation framework to preserve the remedy. In the alternative and without prejudice to the above, the Defendant may contend limitation, but the Plaintiff relies on the stated framework under Article 136 for enforcement of any decree.

In addition to the permanent injunction, the Plaintiff prays, in the alternative and without prejudice to the above, for a temporary injunction under Order 39 Rules 1 and 2 CPC to protect the status quo pending final disposal of the suit, on the basis that the Defendant has commenced encroachment and the Plaintiff is in lawful possession of the schedule property described in Schedule, details of which are set out herein. The facts herein establish that the Plaintiff has been in possession since {{POSSESSION_DATE}} and that the Defendant’s actions commencing on {{ENCROACHMENT_DATE}} threaten the peaceful possession and the Plaintiff’s immediate rights in respect of the schedule property.

Wherever the schedule property is referred to herein, it is described in Schedule attached hereto, and the Plaintiff reserves all rights to seek further relief as may be just and proper upon proof of the encroachment described above. The factual basis for the injunction is that the Plaintiff, being in lawful possession, has been threatened by the Defendant’s assertion of encroachment and interference, and the relief sought is to maintain the status quo and protect peaceable possession until final adjudication of the matter. This section sets forth the legal grounds and applies the statutory provisions to the facts as stated above, with the appropriate alternative care and defense open to the Defendant.

filled_sections.heading: CAUSE OF ACTION
filled_sections.text: Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, is in lawful possession of the schedule property described as being located at {{PROPERTY_ADDRESS}} in {{place_names}}. On {{ENCROACHMENT_DATE}}, Defendant {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} entered onto the property and commenced acts of encroachment intended to disturb Plaintiff’s peaceful possession, thereby threatening Plaintiff’s exclusive occupancy. This initial disturbance gave rise to the right to sue for protection of possession. The cause of action accrued on that date and has continued since, as Defendant has repeated attempts to intrude upon the same property and interfere with Plaintiff’s possession on {{ENCROACHMENT_DATE}} and on subsequent occasions up to the present. The interference is ongoing, and the act remains unresolved; therefore the action is continuing, and Plaintiff prays for a temporary injunction under Order 39 Rules 1 and 2 CPC to restrain Defendant from further intrusion until final disposal, and for a permanent injunction against any future interference thereafter. The schedule property is described herein and may be referred to in the annexures accompanying this plaint.

filled_sections.heading: LIMITATION
filled_sections.text: The suit is brought under Article 136 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963. The period for limitation under Article 136 is {{LIMITATION_PERIOD}}. The cause of action accrued on {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}, the date on which the Defendant first interfered with Plaintiff's peaceful possession of the Schedule property. The suit is within time.

filled_sections.heading: VALUATION AND COURT FEE
filled_sections.text: Valuation and Court Fee: The Plaintiff values the suit for purposes of court fee and jurisdiction at Rs. {{PROPERTY_VALUE}}, being the value of the schedule property described in Schedule A and the possessory relief sought by a permanent injunction restraining the Defendant from interfering with the Plaintiff's peaceful possession. The basis for valuation is the fair market value of the immovable property as on the date of filing. The court fee payable on the plaint is Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}; the court fee has been paid with the filing in the amount of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}.

filled_sections.heading: PRAYER
filled_sections.text: The Plaintiff respectfully prays for relief by way of a permanent injunction and related interim relief in the manner hereinafter set forth. (a) A decree of permanent injunction restraining the Defendant, its servants, agents, and all persons acting for or on its behalf, from directly or indirectly interfering with the Plaintiff's peaceful possession of the schedule property described in Schedule A and Annexure A, and from entering upon the property, obstructing access, ejecting or attempting to eject the Plaintiff, or otherwise disturbing the Plaintiff's possession or enjoyment of the said property. (b) A temporary injunction in terms of Order 39 Rules 1 and 2 CPC restraining the Defendant, its servants, agents, and all persons acting for or on its behalf, from entering upon, encroaching, possessing, or otherwise disturbing the Plaintiff's possession of the schedule property described in Schedule A and Annexure A during the pendency of this suit. (c) Costs of this suit. (d) Any other relief the Court deems fit.

filled_sections.text: The Plaintiff relies upon the documents collectively annexed and marked as Annexures A to F, which are attached to this plaint and pertain to the Schedule Property described in the Schedule, to establish the Plaintiff’s lawful possession and to support the prayers for permanent injunction and for interim relief under Order 39 Rules 1 and 2 CPC. The documents relied upon are: Annexure A — {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}; Annexure B — {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}; Annexure C — {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}; Annexure D — {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}; Annexure E — {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}; Annexure F — {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}.

draft_artifacts.title: IN THE COURT OF THE CIVIL JUDGE AT {{PLACE}}
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE {{COURT_NAME}}
AT {{COURT_PLACE}}

O.S. No. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} / {{SUIT_YEAR}}

IN THE COURT OF THE CIVIL JUDGE AT {{PLACE}}
SUIT FOR PERMANENT INJUNCTION UNDER SECTION 38 OF THE CPC READ WITH ORDER 39 RULES 1 & 2 CPC, IN RESPECT OF SCHEDULED PROPERTY DESCRIBED IN SCHEDULE
PLAINTIFF: {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}; DEFENDANT: {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}


BETWEEN

BETWEEN The Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}, competent to sue and be sued (sui juris), hereinafter referred to as the Plaintiff, and the Defendant, {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}, residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}, competent to be sued and to sue (sui juris), hereinafter referred to as the Defendant. The Plaintiff is in peaceful and lawful possession of the property described in Schedule A hereto. On or about {{ENCROACHMENT_DATE}}, the Defendant attempted to encroach upon and interfere with the Plaintiff's possession of the Schedule A property, thereby rendering continued interference actionable and justifying the grant of a permanent injunction restraining the Defendant; the Plaintiff also prays for a temporary injunction under Order 39 Rules 1 and 2 CPC to maintain the status quo pending disposal of this suit.


JURISDICTION

Territorial jurisdiction for this suit lies with the Civil Court at {{COURT_PLACE}}, {{STATE}}, as the schedule property is situated at {{PROPERTY_ADDRESS}} within this city, and the cause of action arose at the said property due to the Defendant's interference with the Plaintiff's peaceful possession. The Plaintiff has been in lawful possession of the schedule property since {{POSSESSION_DATE}}. The Defendant resides at {{DEFENDANT_RESIDENCE}} within the jurisdiction, and the Plaintiff resides at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}; on or about {{ENCROACHMENT_DATE}} the Defendant began attempting encroachment on the schedule property, located at {{PROPERTY_ADDRESS}}.

The suit, including the temporary injunction prayer under Order 39 Rules 1 and 2 CPC, is maintainable in this Court because the property and the alleged interference occur within the territorial limits of the said Civil Court. The factual nexus—possession by the Plaintiff, encroachment attempts by the Defendant at the schedule property, and the parties’ residences within the same jurisdiction—establishes the appropriate territorial link for adjudication in this Court.

Pecuniary jurisdiction: The suit seeks relief in the nature of a permanent injunction restraining the Defendant from interfering with Plaintiff’s possession and includes a temporary injunction prayer. The suit value is Rs. {{SUIT_VALUE}}, which falls within the pecuniary limits of this Court. The Court’s monetary jurisdiction for such suits is Rs. {{PECUNIARY_LIMIT}}; accordingly, this Court is empowered to entertain and determine the suit on merits.


FACTS OF THE CASE

1. The Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, is the lawful possessor of the schedule property more particularly described in Schedule-A and situated at {{PROPERTY_ADDRESS}}. The Plaintiff has held peaceful possession since before {{DATE_1}}, and there is documentary evidence of title and possession in Annexure A and Annexure B respectively attached to this plaint.

2. On {{DATE_2}}, the Defendant, {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, with others attempted to encroach upon the schedule property by entering within the boundary and erecting unauthorised structures, thereby interfering with the Plaintiff’s peaceful possession. The encroachment is evidenced by the Site Plan in Annexure C and by photographs in Annexure H, which depict the areas showing interference and construction near the boundary.

3. On {{DATE_3}}, the Plaintiff served a legal notice on the Defendant demanding immediate cessation of the encroachment and maintenance of the Plaintiff’s peaceful possession; the notice is annexed to this pleading as Annexure D.

4. On {{DATE_4}}, the Defendant sent a reply disputing the Plaintiff’s possession and asserting rights to the boundary and the alleged encroached portions; the reply is annexed as Annexure E.

5. On {{DATE_5}}, the Plaintiff filed before the appropriate Court an application for temporary injunction under Order 39 Rules 1 and 2 CPC, seeking to restrain the Defendant from interfering with the Plaintiff’s possession pending the hearing of the suit; the application and supporting affidavits are annexed as Annexure F.

6. On {{DATE_6}}, the Court granted ad-interim relief in favour of the Plaintiff by way of temporary injunction restraining the Defendant from interfering with the Plaintiff’s possession, and this interim order is reflected in Annexure G.

7. As of {{DATE_7}}, the Plaintiff continues in peaceful possession of the schedule property, and there has been no restoration of possession to the Defendant or disturbance by the Defendant beyond the interim measures; this status is evidenced by the continued relevance of Annexure B (Possession Certificate) and Annexure H (Photographs) on record.

8. The facts described herein establish a continuing interference with the Plaintiff’s peaceful possession of the schedule property, as described in Schedule-A and situated at {{PROPERTY_ADDRESS}}, which necessitates the permanent injunction prayed for in this suit so as to prevent any further encroachment or disturbance by the Defendant. Annexures A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H are collectively referred to for documentary support of these events.


LEGAL BASIS AND GROUNDS

The Legal Basis and Grounds for the relief sought proceed on the statutory framework governing suits for permanent injunction and the preservation of rightful possession. Under Section 6 of the CPC, as substituted by the CPC (Amendment) Act, 2002 (22 of 2002), when a suit has been duly instituted, a summons may be issued to the defendant to appear and answer the claim and to file the written statement of his defence within thirty days from the day of institution. In the present case, Plaintiff {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}} has duly instituted a suit in the Court of {{COURT_NAME}} at {{LOCATION}} on {{INSTITUTION_DATE}} against Defendant {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} for a permanent injunction restraining interference with Plaintiff’s peaceful possession of the schedule property described in Schedule hereto, as Defendant is alleged to be attempting illegal encroachment. The Court is thus empowered to issue summons to Defendant requiring appearance and defence within the statutory period, enabling the matter to proceed on the merits and for the Plaintiff to obtain final relief restraining further encroachment.

In respect of future enforcement, Article 136 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963 provides the governing framework for enforcement of a decree for permanent injunction after a prolonged period, with the outer time limit of forty years and related considerations. The present suit seeks a permanent injunction to restrain continuing interference with peaceful possession of the schedule property, the description of which is set out in Schedule herein. The legal consequence is that, should a decree for permanent injunction be required and thereafter sought for enforcement, such enforcement would be governed by Article 136 and the corresponding time limits, and the Plaintiff contends that the instant suit is within the applicable limitation framework to preserve the remedy. In the alternative and without prejudice to the above, the Defendant may contend limitation, but the Plaintiff relies on the stated framework under Article 136 for enforcement of any decree.

In addition to the permanent injunction, the Plaintiff prays, in the alternative and without prejudice to the above, for a temporary injunction under Order 39 Rules 1 and 2 CPC to protect the status quo pending final disposal of the suit, on the basis that the Defendant has commenced encroachment and the Plaintiff is in lawful possession of the schedule property described in Schedule, details of which are set out herein. The facts herein establish that the Plaintiff has been in possession since {{POSSESSION_DATE}} and that the Defendant’s actions commencing on {{ENCROACHMENT_DATE}} threaten the peaceful possession and the Plaintiff’s immediate rights in respect of the schedule property.

Wherever the schedule property is referred to herein, it is described in Schedule attached hereto, and the Plaintiff reserves all rights to seek further relief as may be just and proper upon proof of the encroachment described above. The factual basis for the injunction is that the Plaintiff, being in lawful possession, has been threatened by the Defendant’s assertion of encroachment and interference, and the relief sought is to maintain the status quo and protect peaceable possession until final adjudication of the matter. This section sets forth the legal grounds and applies the statutory provisions to the facts as stated above, with the appropriate alternative care and defense open to the Defendant.


CAUSE OF ACTION

Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, is in lawful possession of the schedule property described as being located at {{PROPERTY_ADDRESS}} in {{place_names}}. On {{ENCROACHMENT_DATE}}, Defendant {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} entered onto the property and commenced acts of encroachment intended to disturb Plaintiff’s peaceful possession, thereby threatening Plaintiff’s exclusive occupancy. This initial disturbance gave rise to the right to sue for protection of possession. The cause of action accrued on that date and has continued since, as Defendant has repeated attempts to intrude upon the same property and interfere with Plaintiff’s possession on {{ENCROACHMENT_DATE}} and on subsequent occasions up to the present. The interference is ongoing, and the act remains unresolved; therefore the action is continuing, and Plaintiff prays for a temporary injunction under Order 39 Rules 1 and 2 CPC to restrain Defendant from further intrusion until final disposal, and for a permanent injunction against any future interference thereafter. The schedule property is described herein and may be referred to in the annexures accompanying this plaint.


LIMITATION

The suit is brought under Article 136 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963. The period for limitation under Article 136 is {{LIMITATION_PERIOD}}. The cause of action accrued on {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}, the date on which the Defendant first interfered with Plaintiff's peaceful possession of the Schedule property. The suit is within time.


VALUATION AND COURT FEE

Valuation and Court Fee: The Plaintiff values the suit for purposes of court fee and jurisdiction at Rs. {{PROPERTY_VALUE}}, being the value of the schedule property described in Schedule A and the possessory relief sought by a permanent injunction restraining the Defendant from interfering with the Plaintiff's peaceful possession. The basis for valuation is the fair market value of the immovable property as on the date of filing. The court fee payable on the plaint is Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}; the court fee has been paid with the filing in the amount of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}.


PRAYER

The Plaintiff respectfully prays for relief by way of a permanent injunction and related interim relief in the manner hereinafter set forth. (a) A decree of permanent injunction restraining the Defendant, its servants, agents, and all persons acting for or on its behalf, from directly or indirectly interfering with the Plaintiff's peaceful possession of the schedule property described in Schedule A and Annexure A, and from entering upon the property, obstructing access, ejecting or attempting to eject the Plaintiff, or otherwise disturbing the Plaintiff's possession or enjoyment of the said property. (b) A temporary injunction in terms of Order 39 Rules 1 and 2 CPC restraining the Defendant, its servants, agents, and all persons acting for or on its behalf, from entering upon, encroaching, possessing, or otherwise disturbing the Plaintiff's possession of the schedule property described in Schedule A and Annexure A during the pendency of this suit. (c) Costs of this suit. (d) Any other relief the Court deems fit.


LIST OF DOCUMENTS RELIED UPON

The Plaintiff relies upon the documents collectively annexed and marked as Annexures A to F, which are attached to this plaint and pertain to the Schedule Property described in the Schedule, to establish the Plaintiff’s lawful possession and to support the prayers for permanent injunction and for interim relief under Order 39 Rules 1 and 2 CPC. The documents relied upon are: Annexure A — {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}; Annexure B — {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}; Annexure C — {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}; Annexure D — {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}; Annexure E — {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}; Annexure F — {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}.


VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of the above plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and the contents based on legal advice are believed to be true on the basis of such advice. No part of this plaint is false and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this {{FILING_DATE}}.

As per Order VI Rule 15 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No.: {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
Place: {{COURT_PLACE}}
Date: {{FILING_DATE}}
